content:
{
  "title": "TransPrompt: Cross-Lingual Translation-Enhanced Prompting for Low-Resource Languages",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) often underperform on tasks in low-resource languages due to limited training data and suboptimal prompts. Existing prompting strategies do not sufficiently leverage knowledge from high-resource languages for cross-lingual transfer.",
  "motivation": "Improving LLM performance on multilingual and low-resource tasks is critical for broadening access to NLP technologies. Advances in multilingual LLMs have not yet closed the performance gap for low-resource languages. Recent research suggests that translation and context augmentation can help, but direct integration into prompting remains underexplored.",
  "method": "We propose TransPrompt, a two-stage prompting approach: (1) generate a synthetic prompt in a high-resource language (e.g., English) for the task; (2) automatically back-translate this prompt into the target low-resource language using a neural MT model, and concatenate both the original and translated prompts before the input. The concatenated dual-language prompt provides both rich task context (from the high-resource version) and native language cues, helping the LLM align cross-lingual representations and better understand the task in the low-resource setting. The method can be extended to few-shot or chain-of-thought settings by similarly augmenting examples.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate TransPrompt on standard multilingual benchmarks (e.g., XTREME, FLORES, TyDiQA) focusing on low-resource languages. Baselines include standard monolingual prompting, translation-only prompting, and multilingual chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics will include accuracy, F1, and BLEU (for generation). Ablations will test the impact of prompt translation quality and prompt length."
}
